title: Chicken tikka masala
sources: Colonel A. R. Kenney-Herbert ('Wyvern'), Culinary Jottings for Madras (1878) — public domain — Anglo-Indian curry tradition.
Veeraswamy and other twentieth-century British-Indian restaurant traditions; canonical post-1960s Glasgow/London restaurant origin (oral and trade-press tradition, not a citable source).
notes: British Anglo-Indian classic. Marinated yoghurt-and-spice chicken charred under the grill, then folded into a tomato-cream sauce. The 'national dish of Britain'. ~90 minutes total. Buttery, mild, family-friendly. Make-ahead marinade. Sauce freezes well.
